output: ResourceHacker.exe -open C:\Windows\System32\curl.exe -save C:\Temp\svchost.exe -action modify -res version.res -mask VERSIONINFO,1,1033
explanation: The rule heavily relies on the PE metadata fields 'OriginalFileName' and 'Description'. An attacker can bypass this rule entirely by using a tool like Resource Hacker to strip or modify the Version Info (PE metadata) of the curl.exe binary before executing it under a different name. If the metadata no longer says 'curl.exe', the 'selection' criteria will never trigger.
